board.designation: DB-M1 — Mechanical/HVAC sub-DB, IEC commercial 3-storey office
board.location: rooftop plantroom adjacent to AHU plant

incoming_supply.fed_from: MSB-MAIN F03 (250A MCCB Type D, 45m feeder)

selectivity_results.code_clause: IEC 60364-5-53 § 536 — 250A Type D MCCB upstream selective against ≤32A Type B/C/D MCBs at DB-M1 (≥7.8:1 current ratio at M01/M03 worst case; ≥10:1 on smaller motor circuits). Same-curve (D-D) cascade at M01/M02/M03/M04 supports time-current selectivity per IEC 60898-1; curve separation (D upstream vs B/C downstream) at M05-M08. Engineer-declared per manufacturer cascade table at 14 kA fault level; fault-level skill to confirm full coordination study.

assumptions: IEC 61439-3 Distribution Board (DBO) used for the mechanical sub-DB; IP54 chosen for plantroom dust/moisture exposure.
M01-M06 are 3-phase motor circuits — IEC 60364-4-41 Clause 411.3.3 socket-RCD policy does NOT apply (fixed equipment, no accessible socket).

Motor protection: each motor has a local motor starter/MCC (DOL or VSD) per IEC 60947-4-1 providing thermal overload protection and lock-out isolation. Upstream MCB at DB-M1 provides short-circuit protection only.
Type D MCB curve selected for AHU + extract fan circuits (M01-M04) — handles direct-on-line motor starting inrush (≈6-8× FLC for ≈100ms).
Type C MCB for chilled-water pump (M05) and kitchen extract fan (M06) — softer inrush profile suits Type C.
M07 FCU control panel feed is ELV-class downstream (24V control), but the upstream 230V supply is a small socket-equivalent circuit — 30mA Type A RCD applied.
M08 condensate pump is single-phase, accessible for maintenance — 30mA Type A RCD applied for additional protection per IEC 60364-4-41 Clause 411.3.3 socket-equivalence reasoning.
Estimated PFC at DB-M1 ≈ 14 kA after 45m 50mm² SWA feeder impedance from 25 kA at MSB-MAIN.
10 kA Icn MCBs with cascade backup from upstream 250A MCCB Icu ≥ 25 kA per IEC 60364-5-53 Clause 536.4.

drawing_notes: DB-M1 located in rooftop plantroom adjacent to AHU plant.
Fed from MSB-MAIN F03 via 45m 50mm² 5-core SWA XLPE Cu feeder.
8 final circuits + 4 spare ways (W9-W12 reserved — future VRF expansion, additional FCU bank).
Each motor circuit terminates at a local motor starter/VSD with IEC 60947-4-1 thermal overload protection.
IP54 enclosure for plantroom environment (dust, moisture, oil mist exposure).

rationale.chat_summary: DB-M1: 250A TPN+E mechanical/HVAC sub-DB for the IEC 3-storey office, 8 circuits (4 AHU fan motors + CHW pump + kitchen extract + FCU control + condensate pump), fed from MSB-MAIN F03. Motor short-circuit protection at DB-M1; thermal overload by local starter/VSD per IEC 60947-4-1. Type D MCBs on DOL circuits (M01-M04); Type C on softer-start; 30 mA Type A RCD on M07 control + M08 condensate pump. IP54 plantroom enclosure.

sections.title: Board Identification
sections.summary: DB-M1 is the dedicated mechanical / HVAC sub-DB for the IEC 3-storey commercial office, located in the rooftop plantroom adjacent to the AHU plant. Built as an IEC 61439-3 Distribution Board (DBO) in an IP54 enclosure — IP rating elevated from the indoor IP4X default to handle plantroom dust, moisture, and oil-mist exposure. Role flagged as `mechanical_panel` so downstream SLD, riser, cable-containment, and load-schedule skills recognise it as the mechanical-services distribution and apply motor-circuit diversity rules.

decisions.label: Specialty board role: mechanical_panel
decisions.summary: Flagged on the board block so downstream skills apply motor-circuit diversity and IEC 60364-5-55 motor-protection rules.
decisions.rule: specialty_board role taxonomy — mechanical_panel signals the board as the upstream feed for motor circuits + HVAC plant.

decisions.label: IP54 enclosure for plantroom environment
decisions.summary: IP rating elevated from default IP4X to IP54 to handle plantroom dust, moisture, and oil-mist exposure.
decisions.rule: IEC 60529 IP rating selection — plantroom locations typically require IP5X dust + IPX4 splash protection.

decisions.label: IEC 61439-3 DBO assembly
decisions.summary: IEC 61439-3 Distribution Board class used — DBO appropriate even in a plantroom because the panel is operated by maintenance personnel rather than the general public.
decisions.rule: IEC 61439-3 — Distribution boards intended to be operated by ordinary persons (DBO) — assembly verification requirements.

sections.title: Incoming Supply
sections.summary: 250A TPN+E intake at 400 V fed from MSB-MAIN F03 via a 45 m 50 mm² 5-core SWA XLPE Cu feeder. Declared PFC at DB-M1 is 14.0 kA — attenuated from the MSB-MAIN bus PFC by the 45 m feeder impedance — covered by 10 kA Icn MCBs through cascade backup from the upstream 250A MCCB (Icu ≥ 25 kA). The 250A rating sized for the diversified motor demand: applying motor diversity per IEC 60364-1 Annex C (typically 60-80 % running diversity for HVAC plant, plus the single-largest-motor inrush factor), the design current is ≈90-110 A per phase with significant margin retained for future VRF / FCU expansion. Main switch is a 250A 4-pole switch-disconnector per IEC 60947-3 for emergency manual isolation.

decisions.label: 250A intake sized for diversified motor demand + future VRF margin
decisions.summary: Motor demand diversified per IEC 60364-1 Annex C (~70 % running diversity) gives ≈90-110 A per phase; 250A rating leaves headroom for future VRF / FCU expansion as noted in spare ways.
decisions.rule: Motor circuit diversity per IEC 60364-1 Annex C — full FLC × running diversity factor + largest-motor inrush kicker as the starting-current contributor.

decisions.label: PFC 14.0 kA at DB-M1
decisions.summary: PFC attenuated from MSB-MAIN bus PFC by the 45 m 50 mm² feeder impedance; 10 kA Icn MCBs covered via cascade backup from upstream 250A MCCB Icu ≥ 25 kA.
decisions.rule: Cascade backup permits downstream OCPDs with Icn below the prospective fault current when the upstream device interrupts within its rated Icu and the cascade is grounded in the manufacturer table.

decisions.label: Switch-disconnector main per IEC 60947-3
decisions.summary: Non-OCPD 250A 4-pole switch-disconnector at the DB-M1 main — protection by upstream MSB-MAIN F03.
decisions.rule: Isolation per IEC 60947-3 — main switch provides manual isolation without OCPD function.

sections.title: Circuit Breakdown
sections.summary: 8 circuits: M01/M03 AHU supply fans (11 kW 3-ph, 32A Type D), M02/M04 AHU extract fans (7.5 kW 3-ph, 25A Type D), M05 CHW pump (5.5 kW 3-ph, 20A Type C), M06 kitchen extract (4 kW 3-ph, 16A Type C), M07 FCU/BMS control feed (16A Type B + 30 mA Type A RCD), M08 condensate pump (1.5 kW 1-ph, 10A Type C + 30 mA Type A RCD). Critical engineering point: DB-M1 MCB provides ONLY short-circuit + cable protection — thermal overload + lock-out is local motor starter/VSD per IEC 60947-4-1. Type D on DOL motor circuits rides through the 6-8× FLC starting transient; Type C on softer-start; Type B on the small accessible circuits. M07 + M08 carry RCD because they are accessible / socket-equivalent per § 411.3.3.

decisions.label: Motor protection split: short-circuit at DB-M1, thermal overload at local starter
decisions.summary: DB-M1 MCB sized for short-circuit + cable protection only; thermal overload + lock-out isolation provided by local motor starter or VSD per IEC 60947-4-1 on each motor circuit.
decisions.rule: Motor circuits per IEC 60364-5-55 § 559 — protection split between upstream short-circuit device and local motor starter handling continuous overload + thermal response.

decisions.label: Type D MCB curves on DOL motor circuits (M01-M04)
decisions.summary: Type D curve (Ia ≈ 10-20 × In) chosen for the 11 kW AHU supply fans and 7.5 kW extract fans to ride through the 6-8 × FLC DOL starting inrush (≈100 ms duration) without nuisance tripping.
decisions.rule: MCB curve selection per IEC 60898-1 — Type D suits motor DOL inrush; Type C inadequate at full FLC × 8 factor for the 32A breakers at M01/M03.

decisions.label: Type C MCB on chilled-water pump (M05) + kitchen extract (M06)
decisions.summary: Type C curve adequate on smaller / softer-starting circuits — chilled-water pump exhibits softer inrush than DOL fans; kitchen extract at 4 kW has lower absolute inrush.
decisions.rule: MCB curve selection per IEC 60898-1 — Type C (Ia ≈ 5-10 × In) covers most motor-starting profiles below ≈7.5 kW DOL.

decisions.label: 30 mA Type A RCD on M07 control + M08 condensate pump
decisions.summary: M07 BMS control panel feed includes accessible 230 V supply equipment; M08 condensate pump is a single-phase accessible appliance — both treated as socket-equivalent per § 411.3.3 additional-protection reasoning. Type A selected for SMPS leakage detection.
decisions.rule: IEC 60364-4-41 § 411.3.3 socket-equivalence interpretation — accessible equipment for use by ordinary persons or maintenance personnel benefits from supplementary 30 mA RCD.

decisions.label: No RCD on 3-ph motor circuits (M01-M06)
decisions.summary: Fixed plant equipment without accessible socket-equivalent connection — additional 30 mA RCD not required and would risk nuisance tripping from motor capacitive leakage.
decisions.rule: IEC 60364-4-41 § 411.3.3 additional protection requirement is conditional on socket / accessible-equipment criteria; fixed motor connections at plant are outside scope.

sections.title: Selectivity Analysis
sections.summary: Two-stage cascade: 250A Type D MCCB at MSB-MAIN F03, non-OCPD 250A switch-disconnector at DB-M1, then final MCBs (10-32A Type B/C/D). Worst-case ratio is 250:32 = 7.8:1 at M01/M03 — meets the IEC 60898-1/IEC 60947-2 same-curve (D-D) cascade threshold supported by magnetic-pickup time-current separation between the MCCB and downstream MCBs; remaining circuits operate at 10:1+ ratio with comfortable margin. Curve separation (Type D upstream vs Type B/C downstream) at M05-M08 further widens the selectivity envelope. Verdict pass — engineer-declared per manufacturer cascade table at the 14 kA fault level; fault-level skill to confirm full coordination via the project-graph upstream link.

decisions.label: Worst-case selectivity ratio 7.8:1 at M01/M03
decisions.summary: 250A:32A ratio meets the same-curve (D-D) cascade threshold per IEC 60898-1; the MCCB magnetic-pickup setting sits above the MCB instantaneous trip current, supporting selective behaviour at the 14 kA fault level.
decisions.rule: Same-curve MCCB-to-MCB cascade requires sufficient instantaneous-trip separation; IEC 60364-5-53 § 536 cascade verification via manufacturer table.

decisions.label: Engineer-declared per manufacturer cascade table
decisions.summary: Selectivity declared by engineer at the 14 kA fault level pending formal coordination study from the fault-level skill.
decisions.rule: Engineer declaration is acceptable per IEC 60364-5-53 § 536 when grounded in a manufacturer cascade table; formal IEC 60909-0 + tool calc to be appended at the runtime layer.

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: DB-M1 compliant against IEC 60364-4-41 § 411 (ADS with selective RCD on accessible circuits), IEC 60364-5-52 (cable + voltage drop), IEC 60364-5-53 § 536 (selectivity), IEC 60364-5-55 § 559 (motor circuit protection split), IEC 60947-4-1 (local starter thermal overload), IEC 61439-3 (DBO assembly), and IEC 60529 (IP54 plantroom rating). No non-compliance flags. Motor-protection split is the critical point: the DB-M1 MCB is short-circuit only; the formal motor circuit is complete only when paired with the IEC 60947-4-1 starter declared by the mechanical contractor on the same circuit.

decisions.label: Motor-protection split documented per IEC 60364-5-55 + IEC 60947-4-1
decisions.summary: MCB at DB-M1 = short-circuit + cable protection; thermal overload + lock-out = local starter/VSD per IEC 60947-4-1 declared on each motor circuit.
decisions.rule: Motor circuit protection split per IEC 60364-5-55 § 559 — upstream short-circuit device + local thermal-overload device working as a complementary pair.

decisions.label: ADS achieved per IEC 60364-4-41 § 411
decisions.summary: Disconnection times met by overcurrent device (MCB) — Zs to be verified by cable-sizing skill; 30 mA Type A RCD on M07 + M08 for additional protection per § 411.3.3.
decisions.rule: ADS requirements per IEC 60364-4-41 § 411.3 — disconnection in ≤5 s for distribution circuits ≤63 A and ≤0.4 s for final circuits ≤32 A in TN systems.

decisions.label: IP54 enclosure per IEC 60529 for plantroom
decisions.summary: Enclosure rating raised to IP54 to handle plantroom dust + splash exposure; IEC 61439-3 DBO verification still applicable for the assembly.
decisions.rule: Enclosure IP rating per IEC 60529 selected against installation environment; plantroom defaults to IPx4 splash + IP5x dust minimum.

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Schedule emitted as 8-row table with 4 spare ways (W9-W12) reserved for future VRF expansion and additional FCU banks — typical growth pattern for HVAC-led commercial fit-outs. RCD column populated on M07 + M08; blank on M01-M06 with assumption annotation explaining the fixed-equipment exemption. Motor starter / VSD column annotated on every motor row pointing to the IEC 60947-4-1 device declared by the mechanical contractor. Cable column shows 5-core SWA XLPE Cu (3-ph + N + PE) on the AHU + pump motor circuits; voltage-drop checks on the long-run motor branches to be confirmed by the cable-sizing skill against the IEC 60364-5-52 Annex B 4 % motor-circuit voltage-drop allowance.

decisions.label: 4 spare ways reserved for HVAC expansion
decisions.summary: W9-W12 reserved for future VRF / FCU expansion — typical growth pattern for commercial mechanical fit-outs.
decisions.rule: Design margin per IEC 60364-1 Annex C — spare ways reserved at first installation reduce the cost of foreseeable future expansion.

decisions.label: Motor starter / VSD column annotated on every motor row
decisions.summary: Schedule explicitly identifies the local motor protection device on every motor circuit so the MCB rating is not misread as the complete motor protection.
decisions.rule: Documentation clarity — the motor-protection split must be visible on the schedule to avoid the common error of treating the MCB as the sole motor protection.
